title: Recover a vWii IOS / channel
requirements: A Wii U already running Aroma (a working homebrew setup)
An SD card (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
warnings: This works on the vWii NAND. If misused it can !!destroy vWii!!, so read each mode's description before choosing.
Disable the FTPiiu plugin before starting.
Remove any system update blocks before starting, since you finish with a system update.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body: This fixes a **vWii** (the Wii U's built-in Wii mode) that's broken from a bad softmod, using **vWii Decaffeinator**. You need a working **Aroma** setup, the FTPiiu plugin disabled, and update blocks removed (this ends with a system update).

steps.title: Put vWii Decaffeinator on your SD card
steps.body: Graphite downloads the latest **vWii Decaffeinator** and copies it onto your SD card. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Launch vWii Decaffeinator
steps.body: Power on the Wii U and boot into **Aroma**, then launch **vWii Decaffeinator** from the Wii U Menu.

steps.title: Choose a recovery mode
steps.body:
Pick the least aggressive mode that fixes your problem:

- **Advanced Options**: clears only the IOSes/channels you specify; keeps everything else, including custom IOSes.
- **Light Mode**: reinstalls the System Menu, message board save, IOSes and system titles; **erases custom IOSes** but keeps other data.
- **Aggressive Mode**: clears **everything** back to a clean stock vWii; all custom modifications and saves are lost.

Select your mode and let it run.

steps.title: Finish with a system update
steps.body:
When the app finishes, let it launch **System Settings**, then perform a **system update** to reinstall the clean vWii titles.

(Re-block updates afterwards if you want to keep your Wii U CFW exploits intact.)

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your vWii IOSes/channels are restored. If vWii still misbehaves, re-run a more aggressive mode.
